from playwright.sync_api import sync_playwright
import re

with sync_playwright() as p:
    browser = p.chromium.launch()
    page = browser.new_page()
    
    # 1Password pricing
    page.goto("https://1password.com/pricing/", wait_until="networkidle", timeout=30000)
    text = page.content()
    prices = re.findall(r'\$[\d,]+\.?\d*', text)
    print("=== 1Password (rendered) ===")
    print(f"  Prices: {set(prices)}")
    
    # Look for tier names
    tiers = re.findall(r'(Teams|Business|Enterprise|Individual|Families|Starter)', text)
    print(f"  Tiers: {set(tiers)}")
    
    browser.close()

import requests
import re
import json

headers = {"User-Agent": "Mozilla/5.0 (X11; Linux x86_64) AppleWebKit/537.36"}

# LastPass - look for JSON-LD structured data
r = requests.get("https://lastpass.com/fp/business/", headers=headers)
text = r.text

# Look for JSON-LD
json_ld = re.findall(r'<script type="application/ld\+json">(.*?)</script>', text, re.DOTALL)
for ld in json_ld:
    try:
        data = json.loads(ld)
        print(f"LastPass JSON-LD: {json.dumps(data, indent=2)[:500]}")
    except:
        pass

# Also look for any pricing data in the HTML
from bs4 import BeautifulSoup
soup = BeautifulSoup(text, 'html.parser')

# Look for any element with pricing-related classes
for tag in soup.find_all(class_=re.compile(r'price|pricing|cost', re.IGNORECASE)):
    text_content = tag.get_text(strip=True)
    if text_content:
        print(f"LastPass pricing element: {tag.name}.{tag.get('class')} = {text_content[:200]}")

# Also look for any data attributes with pricing
for tag in soup.find_all(True):
    for attr, val in tag.attrs.items():
        if isinstance(val, str) and 'price' in val.lower():
            print(f"LastPass pricing attr: {tag.name}.{attr} = {val[:200]}")

import requests
import re

headers = {"User-Agent": "Mozilla/5.0 (X11; Linux x86_64) AppleWebKit/537.36"}

r = requests.get("https://web.archive.org/web/2024/https://www.keepersecurity.com/pricing/", headers=headers, timeout=15)
text = r.text

# Find the keeper object initialization
keeper_match = re.search(r'var\s+keeper\s*=\s*\{([^}]*(?:\{[^}]*\}[^}]*)*)\}', text, re.DOTALL)
if keeper_match:
    keeper_obj = keeper_match.group()
    # Look for pricing-related keys
    pricing_keys = re.findall(r'(\w+Cost|\w+Price|\w+User)', keeper_obj)
    print(f"Pricing keys: {set(pricing_keys)}")
    
    # Look for numeric values near pricing keys
    pricing_values = re.findall(r'(\w+Cost|\w+Price|\w+User)\s*:\s*([\d.]+)', keeper_obj)
    for key, val in pricing_values:
        print(f"  {key}: {val}")

# Also look for the full pricing data

import requests
import re
from bs4 import BeautifulSoup

headers = {"User-Agent": "Mozilla/5.0 (X11; Linux x86_64) AppleWebKit/537.36"}

r = requests.get("https://web.archive.org/web/2023/https://lastpass.com/pricing/", headers=headers, timeout=15)
text = r.text

# Look for the template variable values
template_vars = re.findall(r'\{(LP\w+)\}', text)
print(f"Template variables: {set(template_vars)}")

# Look for the values in the HTML
for var in set(template_vars):
    # Search for the variable definition
    pattern = re.compile(rf'{var}\s*[:=]\s*["\']?([\d.]+)', re.IGNORECASE)
    matches = pattern.findall(text)
    if matches:
        print(f"  {var} = {matches}")

# Also look for pricing in script tags
soup = BeautifulSoup(text, 'html.parser')
for script in soup.find_all('script'):
    if script.string and ('LPPremium' in script.string or 'LPBusiness' in script.string):
        print(f"\nScript with pricing:")
        print(script.string[:500])
